name: Lounge Guru
description: Read-only Lounge Guru recipe powered by the hosted lounge-guru MCP. Use it to search, compare, and summarize airport lounge records with source and quality context.

inputContext.label: Primary airport code
inputContext.prompt: Ask for the traveler's main airport as a 3-character IATA code, such as SIN or LAX.

inputContext.label: Airport watchlist
inputContext.prompt: Ask for a saved watchlist of 2 to 5 airports the traveler wants regular lounge updates for.
inputContext.formatHint: List of 2 to 5 IATA airport codes

inputContext.label: Preferred facilities
inputContext.prompt: Ask which facilities matter most, such as showers, food, Wi-Fi, or quiet areas.

inputContext.label: Lounge type preference
inputContext.prompt: Ask whether the traveler prefers LOUNGE, EAT, REST, REFRESH, or UNWIND results by default.
inputContext.formatHint: One of: LOUNGE, EAT, REST, REFRESH, UNWIND

onboarding.prefilledFirstText: Summarize lounge options for my primary airport and include source quality.

required.name: Lounge Guru
required.notes: Public hosted read-only MCP integration for Lounge Guru search, comparisons, and source context.

automations.name: Weekly home airport brief

schedule.description: Every Monday at 8:00 AM in the user's local timezone.

automations.actionText: Summarize lounge options at the user's primary airport, prioritize preferred facilities and lounge type, and include source quality.

automations.name: Weekly watchlist compare

schedule.description: Every Thursday at 8:00 AM in the user's local timezone.

automations.actionText: Compare lounge options across the user's saved airport watchlist, focusing on which airports best match the user's preferred facilities and where the strongest tradeoffs appear.

automations.name: Weekend trip planning brief

schedule.description: Every Friday at 3:00 PM in the user's local timezone.

automations.actionText: Generate a concise planning brief for the top two saved airports in the user's watchlist, with emphasis on terminal differences, lounge type tradeoffs, and facilities that match the user's preferences.

sandboxChecklist: Confirm onboarding asks for primary airport, airport watchlist, preferred facilities, and optional lounge type preference.
Confirm the prefilled first message triggers a useful airport-specific lounge summary.
Confirm the required integration points at the hosted MCP endpoint with streamable-http transport.
Confirm all three traveler-facing automations are present and use the intended schedules and action text.
Confirm sandbox chats stay read-only and grounded in catalog data only.
manualFlow: Run npx poke@latest mcp add https://lounge-guru-mcp.dev-4ee.workers.dev/mcp -n "Lounge Guru".
Open https://poke.com/kitchen and create a new recipe draft.
Copy the fields from this recipe draft into Kitchen.
Test the draft in Sandbox before publishing.
postPublish: Capture the published recipe share link or partner slug.
Record the published link in release notes or maintainer docs.
Keep future edits in the repo draft first, then apply them manually in a new Kitchen draft because published recipes are locked.
